top_5_events: TSMC announces $165B total US investment — largest foreign manufacturing commitment in US history
NVIDIA reports $215.9B FY2026 revenue (114% YoY), projects $1T AI infrastructure cycle at GTC
Waymo raises record $16B, validating autonomous vehicle economics at $92B valuation
China bans rare earth exports to Japan; US responds with 54-nation minerals ministerial
Boston Dynamics launches commercial Atlas + DeepMind partnership for robot foundation models
recurring_themes: Humanoid robotics investment wave: 12+ companies raised $100M+ in Q1
AI compute explosion: NVIDIA $215.9B, TSMC records, CoWoS 3x, SK Hynix $8B EUV
Semiconductor reshoring: TSMC $165B Arizona, Intel 18A, FANUC Michigan
Critical minerals disruption: China export bans, US/EU policy responses
Physical AI emergence: NVIDIA Isaac/Cosmos, Japan $2B strategy, Figure at White House
Export controls evolution: BIS case-by-case framework, Korea annual licenses

month_by_month.january: CES 2026 dominated with major launches (NVIDIA Rubin, Intel 18A, AMD MI400, Boston Dynamics Atlas). TSMC/ASML reported records. BIS finalized AI chip exports. China banned rare earth exports to Japan.
month_by_month.february: Massive funding rounds: Waymo $16B, Apptronik $520M, Wayve $1.5B. NVIDIA reported $215.9B FY2026. MP Materials selected Texas for $1.25B magnet campus. US hosted 54-nation minerals ministerial.
month_by_month.march: TSMC announced $100B additional US investment ($165B total). NVIDIA GTC launched Isaac GR00T N1 and Cosmos 2.0. Tesla showed Optimus Gen 3. Japan $2B Physical AI strategy. EU Chips Act 2.0 proposed.

events.title: SpaceX Opens 2026 with Italian Cosmo-SkyMed Earth Observation Satellite Launch
events.summary: SpaceX launched a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base carrying the COSMO-SkyMed Second Generation Flight Model 3 (CSG-FM3) for the Italian Space Agency and Ministry of Defence. The 1,700-kg synthetic aperture radar satellite will study Earth from 620 km altitude in all weather conditions, marking the first orbital launch of 2026 globally.

events.title: IFR reports record 4.28M industrial robots deployed globally
events.summary: IFR World Robotics report: 4.28M industrial robots deployed, a record. Asia 75% of installations, China 51%.
events.cross_sector_impact: Industrial robot density drives semiconductor sensor demand.

events.title: TSMC reports record Q4 2025 revenue of $26.9B
events.summary: TSMC reported Q4 2025 revenue of $26.9B, up 39% YoY, driven by AI chip demand. Full-year 2025 revenue reached $91.2B.
events.cross_sector_impact: Record foundry revenue signals sustained AI chip demand driving robotics compute and space satellite processing.

events.title: China Bans Dual-Use and Rare Earth Exports to Japan Over Taiwan Tensions
events.summary: China's Ministry of Commerce prohibited exports of all dual-use items to Japan for military end users, including rare earth elements and permanent magnets, in retaliation for Japanese PM Takaichi's comments on Taiwan defense. Japan relies on China for 63% of its rare earth imports. The ban covers approximately 1,100 items across seven categories of medium and heavy rare earths.
events.cross_sector_impact: Disrupts Japanese defense and electronics supply chains; boosts Western rare earth producers like MP Materials; raises urgency for allied mineral diversification strategies.

events.title: NVIDIA unveils Rubin architecture and Blackwell Ultra at CES
events.summary: At CES 2026, NVIDIA previewed Rubin architecture and announced Blackwell Ultra B300 shipments ahead of schedule. Jensen Huang projected $1T in AI infrastructure orders.
events.cross_sector_impact: Rubin and Blackwell Ultra power robotics foundation models and autonomous systems.

events.title: Intel showcases Panther Lake on 18A process at CES
events.summary: Intel demonstrated Panther Lake client processors on 18A process at CES 2026, validating its foundry roadmap. First public silicon on 18A.
events.cross_sector_impact: Intel 18A viability is critical for US semiconductor sovereignty.

events.title: AMD announces MI400 AI accelerator at CES 2026
events.summary: AMD unveiled Instinct MI400 series AI accelerator at CES 2026 with enhanced HBM4 integration and new CDNA architecture.
events.cross_sector_impact: AMD competition broadens compute supply base for robotics development.

events.title: Boston Dynamics unveils commercial Atlas at CES 2026
events.summary: Boston Dynamics debuted commercial all-electric Atlas humanoid robot at CES 2026 for warehouse and manufacturing tasks.
events.cross_sector_impact: Creates pull-through demand for actuators, sensors, and AI compute chips.

events.title: Boston Dynamics + DeepMind partnership for robot foundation models
events.summary: BD and Google DeepMind partnered to develop robot foundation models, combining BD hardware with Gemini Robotics AI.
events.cross_sector_impact: Foundation models require massive GPU compute, driving semiconductor demand.

events.title: Hyundai commits $26B to robotics and future mobility
events.summary: Hyundai announced $26B investment in robotics and future mobility at CES 2026 with Boston Dynamics central.
events.cross_sector_impact: Largest single corporate robotics commitment.

events.title: SEMI Industry Strategy Symposium 2026 Convenes on $1T Semiconductor Path
events.summary: The SEMI ISS 2026 symposium convened January 11-14 at Half Moon Bay, California, gathering semiconductor industry executives to chart strategy toward a $1 trillion market. Sessions addressed sustainable semiconductor materials, wafer bonding as a cornerstone of future architectures, and supply chain adaptation amid geopolitical disruption.
events.cross_sector_impact: Sets strategic direction for semiconductor material demand that impacts all technology sectors including robotics and space systems.

events.title: BIS finalizes AI chip export framework with case-by-case reviews
events.summary: BIS finalized AI Diffusion Rule, moving from blanket denial to case-by-case licensing for AI chip exports to China. Annual license renewals required for Korean chipmakers' China fabs.
events.cross_sector_impact: Export controls reshape global semiconductor supply chains.

events.title: Wolfspeed Produces World's First 300mm Single-Crystal Silicon Carbide Wafer
events.summary: Wolfspeed announced production of a single-crystal 300mm (12-inch) silicon carbide wafer, a major milestone in SiC technology backed by over 2,300 patents. The breakthrough enables next-generation AI infrastructure, AR/VR systems, and advanced power devices by matching the 300mm standard used in traditional silicon manufacturing for improved scalability.
events.cross_sector_impact: 300mm SiC enables higher-efficiency power electronics for robotics, space systems, and EV powertrains; accelerates AI data center power supply capabilities.

events.title: 1X Technologies launches world model for NEO humanoid
events.summary: Norwegian 1X Technologies released a world model for NEO humanoid performing complex manipulation from natural language.

events.title: Trump Signs Critical Minerals Executive Order Under Section 232
events.summary: President Trump signed an executive order titled 'Adjusting Imports of Processed Critical Minerals and Their Derivative Products into the United States' after a Section 232 investigation. Rather than imposing immediate tariffs, the order directs negotiations considering price floors for critical minerals, with a 180-day reporting deadline. The US is fully import-dependent for 12 critical minerals.
events.cross_sector_impact: Affects robotics and semiconductor supply chains dependent on rare earths, tungsten, and specialty metals; potential price floors would reshape global mineral economics.

events.title: ASML reports record Q4 2025 orders of EUR 13.2B
events.summary: ASML reported record Q4 2025 net bookings of EUR 13.2B driven by EUV orders. 2026 outlook of EUR 30-35B.
events.cross_sector_impact: Record EUV orders signal sustained capacity expansion across foundries.

events.title: TSMC plans to triple CoWoS capacity by end of 2026
events.summary: TSMC revealed plans to triple CoWoS advanced packaging capacity by year-end 2026, investing over $10B for AI chip demand.
events.cross_sector_impact: CoWoS capacity is the primary bottleneck gating robotics compute availability.

events.title: NVIDIA ships Blackwell Ultra B300 to hyperscalers
events.summary: NVIDIA began volume shipments of Blackwell Ultra B300 GPUs ahead of schedule. B300 offers 2x inference performance over B200.
events.cross_sector_impact: Accelerated B300 availability speeds deployment of physical AI models.

events.title: ABB integrates NVIDIA Omniverse for digital twin robotics
events.summary: ABB announced full NVIDIA Omniverse integration for physics-accurate digital twin simulation in robot programming.
events.cross_sector_impact: Digital twins reduce deployment time, accelerating manufacturing adoption.

events.title: Rocket Lab Neutron Suffers Stage 1 Tank Rupture During Testing
events.summary: Rocket Lab's Neutron rocket Stage 1 propellant tank ruptured during hydrostatic pressure testing at the company's Space Structures Complex in Middle River, Maryland. A manufacturing fault caused the failure, destroying the test article but causing no facility damage. The incident pushed Neutron's debut launch from Q1 to no earlier than Q4 2026.

events.title: Lithium Carbonate Futures Hit Two-Year High at CNY 180,000/Tonne
events.summary: Lithium carbonate futures surged to CNY 180,000 per tonne, a two-year high, driven by tightening global supplies, export disruptions from Zimbabwe, and front-running by Chinese battery makers ahead of April VAT rebate cuts. Battery-grade lithium carbonate and hydroxide jumped above US$20,000/mt, while spodumene climbed above US$2,000/mt for the first time since October 2023.
events.cross_sector_impact: Higher lithium costs raise input prices for EV batteries and energy storage systems powering robotics and space ground infrastructure.

events.title: Intuitive Surgical reaches 20M cumulative da Vinci procedures
events.summary: Intuitive Surgical hit 20 million cumulative procedures globally. Growth accelerated with da Vinci 5 platform.

events.title: NASA Awards Axiom Space Fifth Private Astronaut Mission to ISS
events.summary: NASA awarded Axiom Space its fifth Private Astronaut Mission (PAM) to the International Space Station, with a launch targeted for January 2027 on SpaceX Crew Dragon. Axiom will propose a four-person crew for the mission, continuing its role as the primary commercial operator of ISS private missions as the station approaches decommissioning.

events.title: AMD reports Q4 data center revenue up 69% YoY
events.summary: AMD reported Q4 2025 revenue of $7.7B with data center up 69% to $4.1B. FY2025 data center exceeded $14B.

events.title: US Hosts Critical Minerals Ministerial; Launches FORGE Alliance and Project Vault
events.summary: The US hosted representatives from 54 countries at the Critical Minerals Ministerial in Washington. Secretary Rubio announced FORGE (Forum on Resource Geostrategic Engagement) to replace the Minerals Security Partnership, with coordinated price floors to counter Chinese market manipulation. President Trump also announced Project Vault, a $10 billion domestic strategic mineral reserve backed by EXIM financing.
events.cross_sector_impact: FORGE's price floor mechanism and Project Vault stockpile directly affect semiconductor and robotics supply chains dependent on rare earths, tungsten, and antimony.

events.title: Waymo raises record $16B at $92B valuation
events.summary: Waymo raised $16B, the largest robotics-adjacent round, at ~$92B valuation. Expanding autonomous ride-hailing to new US cities.
events.cross_sector_impact: Validates AV economics, driving sensor and compute chip demand.

events.title: Inaugural Ariane 64 Launches 32 Amazon Leo Satellites from Kourou
events.summary: The Ariane 64 rocket, the four-booster variant of Ariane 6, completed its maiden flight at 16:45 UTC from French Guiana, deploying 32 satellites for Amazon's Leo constellation (formerly Project Kuiper). Capable of placing 20 tonnes to LEO, the Ariane 64 is the most powerful European launcher, marking the first of 18 Ariane 6 launches contracted for Amazon Leo.

events.title: NASA Awards Vast Space First-Ever Non-Axiom Private Astronaut ISS Mission
events.summary: NASA selected Vast Space for the sixth Private Astronaut Mission to the ISS, marking the first time a company other than Axiom Space received a PAM award. The mission will send a four-person crew to the station no earlier than mid-2027 for a two-week stay, broadening the commercial operator base for ISS access.

events.title: Symbotic acquires Fox Robotics for warehouse automation
events.summary: Symbotic acquired Fox Robotics, adding autonomous forklift capabilities to its warehouse platform.

events.title: Apptronik raises $520M Series B for Apollo humanoid
events.summary: Apptronik raised $520M led by Google with Mercedes-Benz for Apollo humanoid robot production.
events.cross_sector_impact: $500M+ humanoid rounds signal physical AI investment inflection.

events.title: XPeng announces IRON humanoid robot mass production factory
events.summary: XPeng Robotics unveiled Guangzhou humanoid factory targeting 100,000 units/year by 2028.
events.cross_sector_impact: Chinese mass production could reshape pricing and component demand.

events.title: NVIDIA reports FY2026 revenue of $215.9B, up 114% YoY
events.summary: NVIDIA reported FY2026 revenue of $215.9B, more than doubling YoY. Data center hit $180B. Guided Q1 FY2027 at $43B.
events.cross_sector_impact: NVIDIA's scale validates multi-year AI infrastructure investment cycle.

events.title: Intel discloses $15B+ foundry backlog at investor day
events.summary: Intel disclosed $15B+ external foundry commitments. 18A on track for volume H2 2026.
events.cross_sector_impact: Intel foundry diversifies manufacturing away from Taiwan dependence.

events.title: MP Materials Selects Texas for $1.25B Rare Earth Magnet Manufacturing Campus
events.summary: MP Materials announced selection of a 120-acre site in Northlake, Texas, for its '10X' rare earth magnet manufacturing campus, representing $1.25 billion in capital investment and 1,500 jobs. Once operational, the facility will produce 7,000 metric tons of NdFeB magnets annually, bringing total capacity to 10,000 mt/year. Commissioning is set for 2028.
events.cross_sector_impact: Reduces US dependence on Chinese rare earth magnets critical for robotics actuators, satellite reaction wheels, and EV motors.

events.title: Wayve raises $1.5B Series D for embodied AI driving
events.summary: London Wayve raised $1.5B from SoftBank and Microsoft for embodied AI autonomous driving approach.
events.cross_sector_impact: Embodied AI requires massive GPU compute for training.

events.title: TSMC announces $100B additional US investment, $165B total
events.summary: TSMC announced $100B additional US investment, totaling $165B across 6 Arizona fabs. Advanced 2nm production planned 2028. Capex guided $52-56B for 2026.
events.cross_sector_impact: Largest foreign investment in US manufacturing history, reshaping semiconductor supply chain geography.

events.title: TSMC Announces $100B Additional US Investment, Bringing Total to $165B
events.summary: TSMC announced an additional $100 billion investment in US semiconductor manufacturing at the White House with President Trump, bringing total US commitment to $165 billion. The expansion includes three new fabs, two advanced packaging facilities, and a major R&D center in Arizona, supporting 40,000 construction jobs. TSMC's US capacity is fully booked through 2026.
events.cross_sector_impact: Massively expands domestic semiconductor supply chain; drives demand for silicon wafers, specialty chemicals, and materials from ENTG, LIN, APD suppliers.

events.title: SiC Wafer Capacity Set to Surge Globally with Major Fab Ramp-Ups
events.summary: TrendForce reported that 2026 marks a pivotal year for large-scale 200mm SiC manufacturing, with Wolfspeed fully shifting to its 200mm Mohawk Valley fab, Infineon ramping Module 3 at Kulim Malaysia, and STMicroelectronics commencing production at its Catania Italy facility. Chinese capacity is also expanding significantly with multiple 8-inch production lines coming online.
events.cross_sector_impact: SiC supply expansion benefits power electronics for EV, robotics, and space applications; price pressure from Chinese capacity could reshape competitive dynamics.

events.title: Mind Robotics raises $500M for industrial humanoid deployment
events.summary: Shanghai Mind Robotics raised $500M to deploy industrial humanoids in Chinese factories.

events.title: Vast Space Raises $500M to Accelerate Haven Space Station Development
events.summary: Vast Space closed a $500 million round comprising $300 million in Series A equity and $200 million in debt, led by Balerion Space Ventures with participation from IQT, Qatar Investment Authority, Mitsui, and others. Funds will accelerate development of Haven-2, the proposed ISS successor, while Haven-1 targets an early 2027 launch aboard SpaceX Falcon 9.

events.title: Broadcom reports AI revenue doubles, custom ASIC expanding
events.summary: Broadcom reported Q1 FY2026 revenue of $14.9B with AI revenue doubling to $4.4B. Custom ASIC business expanding with 3 hyperscaler customers.

events.title: Tesla showcases Optimus Gen 3 at Shanghai Gigafactory
events.summary: Tesla demonstrated Optimus Gen 3 performing autonomous assembly. Targeting 10,000 internal Optimus units by end 2026.
events.cross_sector_impact: Tesla scale could make humanoids cost-competitive faster.

events.title: Wolfspeed Unveils 300mm SiC Platform for AI Data Center Advanced Packaging
events.summary: Wolfspeed announced a 300mm SiC-based platform for next-generation AI data center advanced packaging, extending the January wafer breakthrough into practical applications. The platform enables integration of high-voltage power delivery, advanced thermal solutions, and active interconnects at wafer scale for AI and HPC workloads.
events.cross_sector_impact: Bridges materials and semiconductor sectors; SiC advanced packaging impacts AI infrastructure that underpins autonomous robotics and satellite data processing.

events.title: imec installs first High NA EUV system (ASML EXE:5200)
events.summary: imec installed the world's first operational ASML EXE:5200 High NA EUV system, enabling sub-2nm patterning.
events.cross_sector_impact: High NA EUV enables next-gen AI chips for robotics and space.

events.title: Japan METI announces $2B Physical AI national strategy
events.summary: Japan METI announced $2B Physical AI strategy targeting robotics, automation, and AI-powered manufacturing.
events.cross_sector_impact: Links semiconductor equipment expertise with robotics deployment.

events.title: NASA Administrator Teases Further Artemis Program Updates Ahead of April Launch
events.summary: NASA Administrator provided updates on the Artemis program during an interview, confirming the April 1 target date for the Artemis II crewed lunar flyby and hinting at upcoming decisions for Artemis III and IV mission architectures. The briefing highlighted the growing commercial lunar ecosystem benefiting contractors Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, and Intuitive Machines.

events.title: NVIDIA GTC 2026: $1T AI infra cycle, Vera Rubin for 2027
events.summary: At GTC 2026, Jensen Huang projected $1T AI infrastructure buildout, unveiled Vera Rubin architecture for 2027, expanded Isaac robotics and Cosmos simulation.
events.cross_sector_impact: Isaac and Cosmos platforms directly enable robotics development.

events.title: NVIDIA GTC: Isaac GR00T N1 and Cosmos 2.0 for robotics
events.summary: NVIDIA launched Isaac GR00T N1 foundation model for humanoids and Cosmos 2.0 simulation platform at GTC.
events.cross_sector_impact: NVIDIA robotics stack drives GPU demand spanning semi and robotics.

events.title: Portal Space and Paladin Space Announce Debris Removal as a Service (DRAAS)
events.summary: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space announced a partnership to offer recurring commercial debris removal missions under a Debris Removal as a Service model, targeting a 2027 deployment focused on LEO. The $1.4 billion space debris monitoring and removal market is growing at 7.1% CAGR, driven by over 40,000 tracked objects in orbit.

events.title: SK Hynix places record $8B EUV order with ASML
events.summary: SK Hynix placed record $8B ASML order for HBM4 and advanced DRAM production. Largest single EUV equipment order ever.
events.cross_sector_impact: HBM capacity expansion enables larger AI models for robotics.

events.title: Micron reports revenue of $9.4B, HBM4 sampling begins
events.summary: Micron Q2 FY2026 revenue of $9.4B, DRAM up 47%. Full-year on track for $23.9B. HBM4 sampling with select customers.

events.title: FANUC announces $90M Michigan robotics facility expansion
events.summary: FANUC expanding Michigan campus by 650,000 sq ft. Driven by US reshoring demand from manufacturers.
events.cross_sector_impact: Aligns with CHIPS Act trends: new fabs need industrial robots.

events.title: Intel confirms multiple 18A customer wins at Foundry Direct Connect
events.summary: Intel confirmed external 18A customer design wins and roadmap through 14A at annual Foundry Direct Connect event.
events.cross_sector_impact: Intel foundry reduces single-point failure risk for defense and space.

events.title: EU Commission Releases Chips Act 2.0 Proposal Targeting Lab-to-Fab Gap
events.summary: The European Commission published its Chips Act 2.0 proposal as part of a broader Technological Sovereignty package, building on over EUR 80 billion in chip investments mobilized by the original Act. Key features include emphasis on advanced packaging capabilities, a harmonized EU-wide permitting regime for fab construction, and treating semiconductor facilities as projects of overriding public interest.
events.cross_sector_impact: Expanded EU semiconductor investment drives demand for specialty materials, gases, and chemicals from LIN, APD, ENTG; reshapes global fab competition.

events.title: Intuitive Machines Stock Surges on NASA IM-5 Lunar Lander Contract Win
events.summary: Intuitive Machines shares surged 36.9% following announcement of a larger IM-5 NASA lunar lander contract worth $180.4 million under the CLPS program. The company continues preparing its IM-3 Nova-C lander mission to Reiner Gamma with redundant laser rangefinders and expanded orbital calibration, while building on its position as the only private company to land on the Moon.

events.title: CMR Surgical launches Versius in the US market
events.summary: UK CMR Surgical received FDA clearance for Versius surgical robot, challenging Intuitive Surgical's US dominance.

events.title: Rocket Lab Launches ESA Celeste LEO-PNT Navigation Satellites
events.summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket launched the first two satellites of ESA's Celeste LEO-PNT constellation at 10:14 CET from Mahia, New Zealand. The CubeSats, built by GMV and Thales Alenia Space, will validate a complementary low-Earth orbit navigation layer to enhance Galileo's resilience, testing L- and S-band signals as part of a planned 11-satellite demonstration constellation.

events.title: NASA FY2026 Budget Finalized at $24.4B; Space Force Reaches $57.7B
events.summary: Congress finalized NASA's FY2026 budget at $24.4 billion, with the largest discrepancy from the White House request since 1987 at nearly 30%, after rejecting a proposed 24% cut that would have eliminated key Artemis architecture and climate research. Separately, US Space Force funding reached approximately $57.7 billion across the DoD, driven by the Golden Dome missile defense initiative.
events.cross_sector_impact: Preserved Artemis funding sustains demand across the space industrial base; Space Force growth benefits defense primes LMT, NOC, LHX.
